title: bober-pipeline.js workflow script and RunResultFlusher
description: Author the pure-JS workflow script .claude/workflows/bober-pipeline.js that orchestrates plan→curate→generate→adversarial-panel→reconcile→retry via agent({agentType}) in parity with the TS pipeline stages, owning no truth, skipping resumeCursor.completedSprintNumbers, chunking the panel ≤16, and returning a WorkflowRunResult. Implement the host-side RunResultFlusher.flush that commits a WorkflowRunResult to durable .bober/ state (saveContract/updateContract, appendHistory, updateProgress), stamping ISO timestamps as the only clock source, atomic per file, flushing after each contract for crash-safety. Since live invoke is dormant, the script is authored + parse/lint-checked and the flusher is unit-tested with a synthetic WorkflowRunResult.

successCriteria.description: .claude/workflows/bober-pipeline.js begins with a literal `export const meta = { name, description, phases }` block (pure literal) and exposes a main(args) flow that calls agent({agentType,...}) for planner/curator/generator/evaluator and uses the JS reconcile port from ./lib/reconcile.js; it parses as an ES module (node --check or an import in a test).

The script logic skips contracts whose sprintNumber is in resumeCursor.completedSprintNumbers, runs the evaluator panel per lens with chunking so no fan-out exceeds 16, branches the retry loop on reconcile().passed, and returns a WorkflowRunResult shape (verified by extracting/importing its pure helper functions in a unit test, or by a structural assertion on a dry-run harness).

RunResultFlusher.flush(projectRoot, config, result) persists the spec, each contract (saveContract/updateContract), appends history (appendHistory) and updates progress (updateProgress), stamping ISO timestamps on pendingHistory entries and contract completedAt; it returns a PipelineResult. A unit test with a synthetic WorkflowRunResult asserts the written .bober/ files.

flush is crash-safe: it commits after each contract (not once at end) and is idempotent — re-flushing the same result does not duplicate history beyond the documented commit-by-message idempotency; a unit test re-runs flush and asserts no duplicate contract corruption.
npm run typecheck, npm run build, and the lint command exit 0; the new flusher and script suites are green within npm run test.

nonGoals: Do not execute the script against the live Workflows runtime — it is dormant this release.
Do not implement WorkflowEngine.run/invoke or the eligibility wiring (sprint 6).
Do not let the script perform fs, Date.now, or Math.random — all clock/fs work is host-side in the flusher.
stopConditions: Stop when the script parses and its pure helpers + the flusher pass unit tests and typecheck/build/lint are green.
Stop when changes are confined to .claude/workflows/bober-pipeline.js, src/orchestrator/workflow/flusher.ts, and their tests.
definitionOfDone: A pure-JS bober-pipeline.js script with a literal meta block orchestrates the plan→curate→generate→panel→reconcile→retry stages via agentType, skips completed sprints, chunks the panel ≤16, and returns a WorkflowRunResult owning no truth; the host RunResultFlusher commits that result to durable .bober/ state stamping timestamps, atomic and flush-after-each-contract; script parses, flusher and script-helper unit tests pass, and typecheck/build/lint are green.
assumptions: The script's pure helper functions (e.g. partition lenses into ≤16 chunks, decide retry) can be factored so they are unit-testable without the live agent() runtime.
Testing the script via a structural import + helper-function assertions is sufficient given the dormant runtime; full end-to-end execution is a manual Claude Code check.
RunResultFlusher reuses the same state helpers the TS pipeline uses so artifacts are byte-equivalent.
generatorNotes: Create .claude/workflows/bober-pipeline.js. Start with `export const meta = { name: 'bober-pipeline', description: '...', phases: [{title:'Plan'},{title:'Sprint'}] }` (PURE literal — no variables). Body uses the workflow runtime hooks (agent, parallel, pipeline, phase, log) as documented for Dynamic Workflows. main(args): PLAN → agent({agentType:'bober-planner', model: args.models.planner, schema: <PlanSpec schema ref>}); if needsClarification return early. PER-CONTRACT loop over args.preloadedContracts (or planned sprints), skipping sprintNumbers in args.resumeCursor.completedSprintNumbers: CURATE agent({agentType:'bober-curator'}); RETRY 1..args.knobs.maxIterations: GENERATE agent({agentType:'bober-generator'}); PANEL run args.evaluatorLenses via parallel chunked into groups of ≤16, each agent({agentType:'bober-evaluator', schema: EvalResult}); reconcile with the JS port `import { reconcile } from './lib/reconcile.js'` (timestamp: pass a placeholder like '' — host re-stamps on flush, or pass args-provided sentinel); if reconcile().passed break else feed feedback into next iteration. Return { spec, perSprint:[{contract, finalVerdict, iterationsUsed, outcome, lensVerdicts}], needsClarification, pendingHistory }. Factor pure helpers (chunk(lenses, 16), decideOutcome(...)) into ./lib/ so they can be unit-tested; OR export them from the script for testing. NO fs/Date.now/Math.random in the script. Create src/orchestrator/workflow/flusher.ts: `export class RunResultFlusher { async flush(projectRoot, config, result): Promise<PipelineResult> }` using saveContract/updateContract (sprint-state.ts), appendHistory (history.ts), updateProgress (history.ts), stamping new Date().toISOString() onto pendingHistory entries (the ONLY clock source) and onto contract completed/updated timestamps; flush per-contract in a loop; build and return a PipelineResult (success, spec, completedSprints, failedSprints, duration). Unit-test flusher with a synthetic WorkflowRunResult against a temp .bober dir (real fs, cleanup). Unit-test the script's pure helpers + a `node --check` style parse (or import the script module and assert typeof meta === 'object' and meta.name). Conventional commit: bober(sprint-5): add bober-pipeline.js workflow script + RunResultFlusher.
evaluatorNotes: Confirm .claude/workflows/bober-pipeline.js parses: run `node --check .claude/workflows/bober-pipeline.js` (exit 0) OR confirm a test imports it. Confirm meta is a pure literal with name/description/phases and matches the phase titles used. Inspect the script for fs/Date.now/Math.random — must be none. Confirm the panel is chunked so no parallel fan-out exceeds 16 and completed sprintNumbers are skipped (read the loop). For RunResultFlusher: run its unit test — assert the temp .bober has the spec, contracts with stamped completedAt, history lines appended, progress updated; re-run flush and assert no corruption/duplication. Confirm timestamps are produced ONLY in the flusher (host), never in the script. Run npm run typecheck, npm run build, and lint (exit 0); suites green tolerating only the flaky tool-count baseline. Confirm git diff confined to the script, flusher.ts, and their tests.
